title: Banana Peanut Butter Smoothie Bowl

subs.name: mango chunks (frozen) for a tropical mango smoothie bowl
almond butter or cashew butter
subs.notes: changes the allergen profile; reduce quantity to 1.5 tbsp as tree-nut butters are richer

ingredients.name: coconut milk
visual_checks: add more if needed to blend smoothly
ingredients.notes: add more if needed to blend smoothly

subs.name: cold water or cold milk
subs.notes: water gives a lighter flavour; dairy milk gives a creamier result

subs.name: mango chunks (frozen) for a tropical mango smoothie bowl
almond butter or cashew butter
subs.notes: changes the allergen profile; reduce quantity to 1.5 tbsp as tree-nut butters are richer

steps.text: The night before, peel ripe bananas, slice them into coins and freeze in a zip-lock bag or covered container. Frozen banana is what gives the bowl its thick, creamy consistency — fresh banana will not work as well.

Add the frozen banana pieces, peanut butter and coconut milk to a blender. Blend on high for about 60 seconds, stopping to scrape the sides as needed. The mixture should be very thick — thicker than a smoothie you drink. Add honey if the bananas are not sweet enough.
Pour the thick blend into a chilled bowl immediately. Working quickly before it melts, arrange toppings: oats for crunch, fresh banana slices, and crushed peanuts. Serve at once.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: coconut-milk → cold water or cold milk (water gives a lighter flavour; dairy milk gives a creamier result); peanut → almond butter or cashew butter (changes the allergen profile; reduce quantity to 1.5 tbsp as tree-nut butters are richer); banana → mango chunks (frozen) for a tropical mango smoothie bowl
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Freeze ripe Robusta or Cavendish bananas — available everywhere in India — overnight in a steel container. Use homemade peanut chutney (groundnut paste) or store-bought creamy peanut butter available at most supermarkets. A regular mixie (Indian blender jar) works fine; blend in short pulses to protect the motor when the mixture is very thick.
